rationale: The citing paper’s contribution and methods are behavioral serotonin/tryptophan studies of impulsive choice (Thanksgiving survey + randomized drink experiment), while the cited paper is a commentary on fMRI methodology in attraction-effect research; the notes show no direct overlap in core constructs or methods. The match evidence is only a single DOI reference entry with no contextual quotation or indication of substantive use. This looks most consistent with a generic/background mention rather than a central dependency.
